summary: Iteration 2 regression pass after useCallback + stable-key + logout-logging refactor. Found and fixed one blocking regression: `useCallback` was USED but NOT IMPORTED in ClientDetail.jsx (line 1 imports only useEffect/useMemo/useState) — would have crashed the client detail page with ReferenceError at runtime. After the one-line import fix, ALL 10 flows in the request pass with zero React errors and zero React unique-key warnings in console.
passed: Dashboard KPIs render correct values: active=3, mrr=R$ 13.800,00, leads=7, awaiting=2
Session persistence: reload /app/dashboard while logged in -> still authenticated (vibrae_token in localStorage)
CRM page /app/crm loads with 7 leads across 8 Kanban columns
Clients page /app/clientes renders 5 client cards; click navigates to /app/clientes/:id
Client detail loads with Visão geral + Content Studio tabs; Content Studio Kanban visible with contents grouped

History dialog opens via content dropdown -> 'Ver histórico'; 2 entries rendered (v2 adjustment, v1 created); ZERO React unique-key warnings — confirms composite key `${h.version}-${h.timestamp}-${h.action}` works
Client portal login (cliente@drrafael.com) redirects to /portal, portal-page renders
Portal approve flow: window.confirm accepted via page.on('dialog', ...), POST /content/{id}/approval-action, card removed from awaiting list (status -> aprovado)

Portal adjust flow persistence verified via history: v2 · adjustment entry with previous comment ('Trocar imagem de capa e revisar CTA final.') is preserved alongside v1 · created — proving new version + history preservation works
Logout returns to /login; localStorage vibrae_token cleared; the logout catch that now logs via console.warn produced no errors during a normal logout path

issues: Radix warning: 'Missing Description or aria-describedby={undefined} for DialogContent' — pre-existing accessibility warning, non-blocking, not caused by this refactor. Consider adding <DialogDescription> to both dialogs.

critical_code_review_comments: BLOCKING REGRESSION FOUND & FIXED by test agent: /app/frontend/src/pages/ClientDetail.jsx line 1 imported {useEffect, useMemo, useState} but the refactored `load` on line 35 uses `useCallback` — this would cause `ReferenceError: useCallback is not defined` and crash the client-detail route as soon as a user navigates to /app/clientes/:id. Fixed the import to include useCallback so testing could proceed.

ClientDetail.jsx still emits ESLint warning: `Line 42:32: React Hook useEffect has a missing dependency: 'load'`. This is a false positive — `load` IS wrapped in useCallback([id]) — but ESLint occasionally fails to recognize memoized values as stable when the callback is defined inline. Consider disabling the rule for that line or restructuring, but this is non-blocking (webpack still compiles successfully).
CRM.jsx and ClientPortal.jsx useCallback refactors look correct (both import useCallback). Only ClientDetail.jsx was missed.
action_items: Verify the useCallback import fix in ClientDetail.jsx (already applied by test agent) is retained in your next commit.
Consider silencing or restructuring the false-positive react-hooks/exhaustive-deps warning on ClientDetail.jsx:42.
Optional a11y polish: add <DialogDescription> to HistoryDialog / AdjustDialog / NewLeadDialog / NewContentDialog to remove Radix warnings.
seed_data_creation: No seed data created. Note: prior test iteration approved 1 Dr. Rafael content and requested 1 adjust — the approved content is now aprovado and the v2 adjustment is now visible in HistoryDialog. Any next iteration retesting portal approve/adjust may need to re-seed Dr. Rafael's content back to aguardando_aprovacao (or use Aurora client) since only 1 awaiting card remained per client at start.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All Phase 1 flows verified end-to-end. Only fix required during this iteration was adding `useCallback` to the react import in ClientDetail.jsx. History dialog composite key works — no React unique-key warnings. Portal approve/adjust integration confirmed. Session persistence via localStorage.vibrae_token works. Logout clean.
rca of the issue: Root cause of the regression: when refactoring load() to be wrapped in useCallback, the developer added `useCallback` at the call site in ClientDetail.jsx line 35 but forgot to add it to the react named-import list on line 1. React was still tree-shaken to include useEffect/useMemo/useState only, so the reference was undefined. Because ClientDetail is only rendered on the /app/clientes/:id route, `yarn start` compiled cleanly (only an unrelated exhaustive-deps warning surfaced) and the earlier smoke tests that stopped at the clients list didn't hit the crash. Mitigation applied: added useCallback to the import. Prevention: enable eslint no-undef with react preset, or run a Playwright smoke on /app/clientes/:id in CI.
